back_translation: 'Even today my complaint is bitter; His hand is heavy upon my groaning.
If only I knew where to find Him, I would go to His dwelling place.
I would lay my case before Him and fill my mouth with arguments.
I would learn how He would answer me and understand what He would say to me.
Would He contend with me in His great power? No, He would pay attention to me instead.
There an upright man could reason with Him, and I would be delivered forever from my Judge.
Behold, if I go east, He is not there; if I go west, I cannot find Him.
When He is at work in the north, I cannot see Him; when He turns to the south, I cannot behold Him.
But He knows the way I take; when He has tested me, I will come forth pure as gold.
My feet have followed in His footsteps; I have kept His way without turning aside.
I have not departed from the command of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth more than my daily food.
But He is unchangeable; who can oppose Him? He does whatever His heart desires.
For He carries out what He has decreed for me, and He has many more such plans.
Therefore I am terrified before His face; when I consider this, I fear Him.
God has made my heart faint, and the Almighty has terrified me.
Yet I am not silenced by the darkness, though thick darkness covers my face.
